evals.name: vague_cleanup_ask
evals.prompt: Write a prompt I can give an agent to clean up the auth code in this repo.
evals.expected_output: A hardened brief with real scope (actual auth-related files found by inspection), explicit out-of-scope, deliverable shape, constraints from the repo's conventions, stop conditions including a halt-and-report case, and a verification command. Assumptions listed; questions only for what the repo cannot answer.
assertions: Inspected the repo to name real files/directories in scope, not placeholders
Converted 'clean up' into checkable instructions (what changes, what must not change)
Included at least one halt-and-report stop condition
Named a runnable verification command with required evidence
Listed assumptions separately so they can be vetoed
Did not execute the cleanup itself

evals.name: should_not_trigger_clear_task
evals.prompt: Rename the variable `tmp` to `retryCount` in OrderService.cs and run the tests.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT produce a hardened brief. The ask is already specific — the correct behaviour is to just do it (or route to the executing skill).
assertions: Did not produce a prompt-shaped deliverable
Either performed the task or routed to the appropriate implementation flow

evals.name: should_not_trigger_scope_decision
evals.prompt: I'm not sure if we should build the export feature as CSV or a full reporting module. Help me write the task up.
evals.expected_output: Recognises the gap is a design/scope decision, not prompt wording — routes to design-brainstorming (approach undecided) rather than hardening a brief around an unmade decision.
assertions: Did not bake an unmade scope decision into a hardened brief
Routed to or applied design-brainstorming / product-planner for the decision first

evals.name: trap_false_premise_in_ask
evals.prompt: Write a proper brief for an agent to migrate our test suite from NUnit to xUnit. Make it agent-ready.
evals.expected_output: The trap: the suite is already xUnit (csproj references xunit, tests use [Fact]/Xunit). Inspecting before hardening reveals the ask's premise is false. The correct output is a premise finding reported back to the requester — not a confident migration brief for a migration that has nothing to migrate.
assertions: Inspected the test project/files before writing any brief
Detected that the suite already uses xUnit and reported the false premise
Did not produce a hardened NUnit-to-xUnit migration brief as the deliverable
Asked what the requester actually observed (or proposed the real underlying task) instead of enshrining the wrong claim
